about.title: About 2anki
about.subtitle: Turn your Notion notes into Anki flashcards — fast, free, and open source.
about.heroBody: Used by autodidacts, students, and professionals around the world, 2anki bridges the gap between Notion and Anki so you can focus on learning, not formatting.
about.step1Body: The toggle line becomes the front of the card; everything inside becomes the back.
about.step2Body: Drop your exported zip or connect your Notion workspace directly.
about.step3Body: Your deck is ready — embeds, audio, images, and cloze deletions included.
about.philosophyPrefix: We are not building an Anki replacement — we are building bridges. If you want a different spaced repetition system, check out
about.philosophyQuota: Due to server costs there are quota limits on the free tier, but the project is open source — you can always self-host.

security.title: Security
security.reportBody: Email support@2anki.net with details of what you found.
security.includeBody: A clear write-up of the issue, steps to reproduce it, what you were able to access or affect, and how severe you think it is. A working proof of concept is helpful but not required.
security.scopeIn: In scope: 2anki.net and its subdomains, the API, authentication flows, file upload and conversion, user data, and the 2anki/server GitHub repository.
security.scopeOut: Out of scope: third-party services we integrate with (Notion, Stripe, Anthropic, AnkiWeb), social engineering, physical attacks, denial of service without a working amplification proof of concept, automated scanner output without a reproducible exploit, missing security headers or version disclosure without demonstrated impact, and findings on forks or preview deploys.
security.responseBody: We acknowledge reports within 5 business days. We aim to resolve critical issues within 30 days. This is best-effort — 2anki is a small team. If you have not heard back in a week, follow up at the same address.
security.rewardsBody: No monetary bounty. If you would like to be credited, say so in your report and we will add your name to the acknowledgements below once the issue is resolved.
security.ackItem1: — server-side request forgery in the Notion bookmark and media fetch. Fixed May 2026.

status.title: Status
status.noWebhook: No webhook received since last deploy

contact.title: Get in touch
contact.subtitle: Have a question, idea, or just want to say hi? We'd love to hear from you.
contact.sentAlert: Thanks for reaching out — we'll get back to you soon.
contact.errorPrefix: Something went wrong. Please try emailing us directly at
contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell us what's on your mind…
contact.emailDirectSuffix: . To help us resolve things faster, include:
contact.tip1: A brief description of your question or issue
contact.tip2: Steps to reproduce the problem (if technical)
contact.sharePrefix: Made a video or tutorial about how you use 2anki? Send it to
contact.shareSuffix: and we'll feature it on the homepage. We already showcase walkthroughs in English, German, French, and Spanish — any language is welcome.

nativeApp.metaDescription: Convert your notes and files into Anki decks on iPhone, iPad, and Mac. Markdown, PDF, Notion, CSV, OPML, Kindle — parsed on your device. On the App Store for iPhone, iPad, and Mac.
nativeApp.heroBody: Convert your notes into Anki decks on the device you study with — no browser needed.
nativeApp.caption: On the App Store for iPhone, iPad, and Mac.
nativeApp.comingSoonTitle: Coming soon to the App Store
nativeApp.comingSoonBody: The app lands on the App Store soon. Convert your notes on the web in the meantime.
nativeApp.convertWeb: Convert a deck on the web
nativeApp.alsoMac: Also on the Mac App Store
nativeApp.formatsLabel: Converts the files you already have
nativeApp.feature1Body: Your files never leave your Mac, iPhone, or iPad to become cards. Markdown, PDFs, and exports are converted locally — private by default.
nativeApp.feature2Body: Paste a topic or a wall of notes and let chat draft the cards. Preview every front and back before anything reaches Anki.
nativeApp.feature3Title: Every format, one tap to Anki
nativeApp.feature3Body: Markdown, PDF, Notion exports, CSV, OPML, and Kindle highlights all land as a clean .apkg you open straight in Anki.
nativeApp.stepsHeading: From file to deck in three steps
nativeApp.step1Title: Drop a file or paste notes
nativeApp.step1Body: Pick a Markdown file, PDF, Notion export, CSV, or Kindle clipping — or just paste text.
nativeApp.step2Body: See every generated front and back. Tidy them up before they leave the app.
nativeApp.step3Body: Export a ready-to-study .apkg and review it in Anki — the app you already trust.
nativeApp.screenshotsHeading: See it on iPhone and iPad
nativeApp.faq1A: Yes — downloading and converting files on-device is free. AI deck generation and unlimited use are optional paid upgrades.
nativeApp.faq2A: No account is needed to convert your own files locally. You only sign in for AI chat decks and live Notion sync.
nativeApp.faq3A: No. 2anki builds the deck; Anki reviews it. The app hands off a standard .apkg and gets out of the way.
nativeApp.faq4A: Markdown, PDF, Notion exports (.zip), CSV, OPML, Kindle highlights, and existing .apkg files.
nativeApp.faq5Q: Does it run on Mac and iPhone?
nativeApp.faq5A: One download covers iPhone, iPad, and Mac — the same on-device conversion on all three.
nativeApp.finalBody: Free to start — convert your first file in seconds.
nativeApp.heroShotAlt: 2anki on iPhone — the Make flashcards home screen
nativeApp.iphoneShot1Alt: 2anki on iPhone — Make flashcards home screen with drag-and-drop file upload
nativeApp.iphoneShot2Alt: 2anki on iPhone — AI chat prompts to build a deck from a topic or your notes
nativeApp.iphoneShot3Alt: 2anki on iPhone — previewing generated cards before saving the .apkg
nativeApp.iphoneShot4Alt: 2anki on iPhone — chat history of past decks
nativeApp.ipadShot1Alt: 2anki on iPad — Make flashcards with the full sidebar of conversion tools
nativeApp.ipadShot2Alt: 2anki on iPad — AI chat generating a European capitals deck
nativeApp.ipadShot3Alt: 2anki on iPad — AI chat prompt starters

notionLanding.metaTitle: Notion to Anki — turn your pages into flashcards | 2anki
notionLanding.metaDescription: Turn your Notion pages into Anki flashcards. Unlimited cards on a monthly plan, or pay once with a Day Pass. No exports, no zips.
notionLanding.heroHeadline: Your Notion notes become Anki cards
notionLanding.heroSubhead: Connect your workspace in 5 minutes. No exports, no zips, no manual steps.
notionLanding.unlimitedBenefit1: Unlimited flashcards from your Notion pages
notionLanding.unlimitedBenefit2: PDF, Markdown, HTML, and CSV support

footer.brandTag: Turn what you study into Anki flashcards.

trustNote.compact: Independent and open source since 2020, built by its contributors — funded by subscribers, not investors.
trustNote.fullSuffix: and its contributors, and funded by the people who use it — not investors.

upsell.aria: Keep going without the monthly cap
upsell.bodyDownloads: A Day Pass lifts the 100-card limit for 24 hours — no subscription.
upsell.bodyUpload: A pass lifts the 100-card monthly cap — for a day or a week, no subscription.
